fields.description:
Goals

In this test plan we will be testing the first set of HLR and LLR to ensure that our initial development and test process is correct. We will be using a few real requirements and test cases.

 

Roles and Responsibilities

Software Engineers, Independant testers, the Chief Systems Engineer

and the DER will participate.

 

Participants

Name Role
Becky Carlton SW Engineer
Lance Demeter Test Engineer
Bob Vandenberg Systems Engineer
Marco Gonzales FAA DER

 

Schedule

These tests will be run during business hours as needed.

 

 

Environment(s)

<In this section, describe the environment(s) that will be needed to complete this testing.>
fields.name: Combined SW HLR and LLR Incremental Tests

fields.description:
The Department of Flight's Strategic Plan calls out two goals:

 

Scientific Breakthroughs in Air Operations Technology

Achieve the major scientific discoveries that will drive U.S. competitiveness; inspire America; and revolutionize approaches to the Nation’s use of flight, national security, and air operations challenges.

 

Foundations of Science

Deliver the scientific facilities, train the next generation of scientists and engineers, and provide the laboratory capabilities and infrastructure required for U.S. scientific primacy.

 

 

In support of these missions, the Office of Unmanned Systems mission is to “support fundamental research to understand, predict, and ultimately deliver airborne technology science  in order to provide the foundations for new airborne technologies and to support DOF missions in aerospace, environment, and national security”.
fields.name: Statement of Mission Need

fields.description: The Achiever UAV will be built using a pre-existing aircraft platform provided by Acme Aviation.
fields.name: Standard Aurcraft Platform

fields.description:
The UAV shall be designed so that it can perform multiple mission types with minimal adjustment to the payload.

  • Research and Development
  • Reconnaissance
  • Fire Combat
  • Precision Agriculture
fields.name: Capable of Multiple Mission Types

fields.description:
The ultimate goal in the development of autonomy technology is to replace the human pilot. The UAV shall operate autonomously executing and pre-defined mission program.

The following shall be automated using the latest technologies:

  • Sensor Fusion
  • Communications
  • Motion Planning
  • Trajectory Generation
  • Task Allocation & Scheduling
  • Cooperative Tactics
fields.name: Autonomous Operation

fields.description: Flight plan must receive updates dynamically based on the flight patterns of other UAV's operating in the swarm as well as the completed mission objectives.
fields.name: Dynamic Updates to Flight Plan

fields.description: Flight operation and mission operation performed by a maximum of 2 operators. The FCS will enable the UAV operator to communicate, receive mission tasking, conduct mission planning, execute the mission, collect, process, and disseminate data for the TUAV and MAE UAV (threshold) and support data collection from HAE UAV.
fields.name: Max 2 Person Remote Control
Swarming

fields.name: plan2

fields.description: Ensure that the chosen platform is designed to carry out multiple types of mission scenarios.
fields.name: Multiple Mission Capability

fields.description: The standard platform from ACME UAV Industries shall be used.
fields.name: Achiever UAV Platform

fields.description: The Tactical Control System (TCS) is the software, software-related hardware and the extra ground support hardware (antennae, cabling, etc.) necessary for the control of the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV). The TCS will also provide connectivity to identified Command, Control, Communications, Computers , and Intelligence (C4I) systems. TCS will have the objective capability of receiving High Altitude Endurance (HAE) UAV payload information. Although developed as a total package, the TCS will have the capability to be configured and down- scaled to meet the user's deployability or operator limitations.
fields.name: Remotely Controlled Tactical Control System (TCS)

fields.description: Drag shall be calculated by XR57
fields.name: Drag calculation

fields.description: Error correction shall be performed by using CFEDC
fields.name: Error correction
UAV does not follow any deleted flight paths
UAV communicates current and stored flight paths back to ground station

fields.description: The CL shall read the FCU RVDT Voltage Feedback [OSIN_FCU_RVDT_VFBK] in engineering unit (mV) from the Operating System.
fields.name: CL read

fields.description: The CL shall input the FCU RVDT Voltage Feedback [OSIN_FCU_RVDT_VFBK] into a 2 dimensional map [MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG] to determine the FCU Feedback Angular Position [FCU_RVDT_ANG] in degrees.
fields.name: CL input

fields.description: The CL shall define the 2 dimensional curve (FCU RVDT Voltage Feedback to FCU RVDT Angular Position) [MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG] as per a the following (x,y) points.
fields.name: CL define

fields.description: The CL shall range limit the output of the 2 dimensional curve (FCU RVDT Voltage Feedback to FCU RVDT Angular Position) [MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG] between the minimum y value and maximum y value defined in the map.
fields.name: CL range limit

fields.description:
The CL shall calculate the FCU RVDT Position Error [FCU_RVDT_POS_ERR] as per the following equation:

 

FCU Position Error = FCU Position Setpoint - FCU RVDT Angular Position
fields.name: CL calculate

fields.description: The FCU RVDT Voltage Feedback Current Value in engineering unit (mV) [OSIN_FCU_RVDT_VFBK.k] shall be read from the Operating System analog input array IN_OS_ANLG[0].
fields.name: Read Function

fields.description: The FCU RVDT Voltage Feedback Last Past Value in engineering unit (mV) [OSIN_FCU_RVDT_VFBK.k1] shall be equal to its Current Value.
fields.name: Input

fields.description:
The FCU RVDT Voltage to FCU Angular Position Map [MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG] shall be defined by the following points :

MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG.x1=-14.6;
MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG.y1=12;
MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG.x2=187;
MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG.y2=-98;
fields.name: Define

fields.description: The FCU RVDT Angular Position low and high values [RNGLIM_FCU_RVDT_ANG] shall be defined as the minimum and maximum value of thein the for The FCU RVDT Voltage to FCU Angular Position Map [MAP2D_FCU_RVDT_MV_TO_DEG].
fields.name: range limit

fields.description: The requirement text here.
fields.name: Interrupt request input
